title: andropogon ivohibensis
intro: Andropogon ivohibensis, also known as Sakay Roa or Madagascar Grass, is a type of grass native to the island of Madagascar, belonging to the Poaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Andropogon ivohibensis include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil for its medicinal uses.
